---
title: "Week 1 — Conceive"
description: "User research, concept selection, and personality brief"
---

```{=html}
<div class="week-hero week-hero-w1">
  <div class="week-num" style="color:#60A5FA">// WEEK ONE &nbsp;·&nbsp; 第一周</div>
  <div class="week-title">Conceive</div>
  <div class="week-zh">构思</div>
  <p class="week-desc">
    Before you touch a single component, you need to understand the person your robot will serve.
    Week 1 is about <strong>listening, asking, and imagining</strong> — turning a vague idea into a focused concept.
  </p>
</div>
```

## Learning Objectives

By the end of Week 1 you will be able to:

- Conduct basic user research interviews and synthesize findings
- Define a target user and a genuine unmet need
- Articulate the **personality** and **purpose** of your robot in a one-page concept brief
- Sketch interaction scenarios and rough form factors
- Present your concept for peer critique and iteration

---

## Sessions

→ [View period-by-period schedule](schedule.qmd)

### Session 1 — Monday · Kick-off, Character Design & Concept Brief

The course begins. Guest lecturer JunJun Shi leads the afternoon — students leave with a team, a concept direction, and a clear sense of what robot personality means.

**Agenda:**
- Course overview and grading (Pass / No Pass) · 梁俊睿
- Embedded systems & IoT fundamentals · meet the ESP32 · 梁俊睿
- **Guest lecture:** JunJun Shi — *"Empathy as the starting point for designing robot personality"* · character design workshop with sketching & LLM generative tools (2 periods)
- **Brief poster session** — all teams present their robot concept (3 min each)
- Industrial design intro: form follows function · sketching · Daniel

---

### Session 2 — Wednesday · ESP32, Firmware & Ideation

Hands-on from the start. By the end of the session your board is talking to an LLM.

::: {.callout-tip title="Design Thinking in action"}
The goal of user research is not to confirm your assumptions — it's to **break them**. The most interesting insights come from unexpected answers.
:::

**Agenda:**
- LLMs & conversational AI fundamentals · 梁俊睿
- ESP-Claw framework overview · 梁俊睿
- ESP32 first contact: hardware setup, GPIO, breadboard wiring · Shimon + Daniel
- Firmware lab: Hello World — display output & LED blink · Shimon + Daniel
- Robot concept ideation workshop: user persona, target need · Shimon + Daniel
- Open studio: form exploration & sketching · Daniel

---

## The Robot Personality Brief

This is the key output of Week 1. It answers these questions in one page:

| Question | What to define |
|---|---|
| Who is it for? | Specific user + context |
| What problem does it solve? | The core unmet need |
| What does it *feel* like to interact with? | Personality adjectives (3–5) |
| What does it say / how does it speak? | Tone, language, style |
| What does it look like? | Sketch or moodboard |
| What does it *not* do? | Explicit non-goals |

::: {.callout-important title="Keep it on one page"}
The constraint is intentional. If you can't summarize your concept in one page, you don't have a concept yet — you have a wish list.
:::

---

::: {.deliverables}
#### ✓ Week 1 Deliverables

- [ ] **User Persona** — one documented user with a real need (from your interview)
- [ ] **Robot Concept Brief** — 1-page: user, need, personality, rough form factor
- [ ] **3 concept sketches** — rough drawings, one per team member
- [ ] **Team roles defined** — who leads design, firmware, fabrication, pitch
:::

---

## Resources for Week 1

**Design Thinking & User Research**

- [Stanford d.school Design Thinking Bootcamp Bootleg](https://dschool.stanford.edu/resources/the-bootcamp-bootleg) — the canonical guide to empathy and concept work
- [Interview for Empathy — Stanford d.school method card](http://dschool-old.stanford.edu/wp-content/themes/dschool/method-cards/interview-for-empathy.pdf) — one-page field-ready guide for empathy interviews
- [User Interviews 101 — Nielsen Norman Group](https://www.nngroup.com/articles/user-interviews/) — planning, conducting, and extracting insight from user interviews
- [IDEO Human-Centered Design toolkit](https://www.designkit.org) — broader design research methods

**Robot Character & Personality**

- [Almost Human: How to Write Robot and AI Characters](https://www.servicescape.com/blog/almost-human-how-to-write-robot-and-ai-characters) — personality traits, speech patterns, and belief systems for AI characters
- [Designing Personas for Expressive Robots — ACM THRI](https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/3424153) — peer-reviewed research on building robot personas through speech, color, and movement

**Getting Started with ESP32**

- [Getting Started — Arduino ESP32 (Espressif official)](https://docs.espressif.com/projects/arduino-esp32/en/latest/getting_started.html) — easiest on-ramp for students new to ESP32
- [Getting Started with ESP32 — Random Nerd Tutorials](https://randomnerdtutorials.com/getting-started-with-esp32/) — step-by-step setup, pinout, and first sketch
- [ESP-Claw documentation](https://esp-claw.com/en/) — start getting familiar early

---

*Next: [Week 2 — Design →](week2.qmd)*
